# KEV Analysis

> Empirical analysis of which security vulnerabilities actually get exploited in the wild, and which don't. Built from the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog and NVD critical/high CVEs, with network-parsing components identified as a 3-6x exploitation predictor at every CVSS severity level.

This file is a machine-readable summary intended for LLMs, scrapers, and other non-interactive consumers. The interactive HTML pages compute their numbers from the same DATA blob at page load; this file mirrors the latest values as a static snapshot, regenerated by the nightly refresh agent. Last updated: 2026-04-25 (catalog version 2026.04.23).

## Headline numbers (current)

- 1,579 total CISA KEV entries
- 887 of those target CVEs published 2021-2026 (the analysis window)
- 692 additional KEV entries target older CVEs (excluded from rates)
- 23,501 NVD critical/high CVEs published 2021-2026 (the denominator)
- 3.8% overall exploitation rate
- 78.2% OS exploitation rate (highest layer; the NVD-321 denominator may be narrower than the KEV os bucket which sweeps Microsoft, Apple, Android, and Linux)
- 0.62% firmware/hardware exploitation rate (23 of 3,739)
- 100.0% email/collaboration server rate (44/44; small NVD denominator, treat as upper-bound artifact)
- 186 windowed KEV entries linked to known ransomware campaigns (~21% of windowed total)
- 3-6x lift for network-parsing CVEs vs. non-parsing CVEs at the same CVSS severity (the central thesis)

## Per-layer exploitation (windowed 2021-2026)

| Layer | KEV | NVD | Rate |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| email_collab_server | 44 | 44 | 100.00% |
| os | 251 | 321 | 78.19% |
| iot_ics | 22 | 92 | 23.91% |
| productivity_desktop | 21 | 129 | 16.28% |
| vpn_network_appliance | 130 | 804 | 16.17% |
| cms_webapp | 10 | 70 | 14.29% |
| virtualization_container | 14 | 111 | 12.61% |
| database | 5 | 48 | 10.42% |
| browser | 56 | 601 | 9.32% |
| jvm_runtime | 25 | 348 | 7.18% |
| web_server | 3 | 47 | 6.38% |
| library_framework | 4 | 80 | 5.00% |
| firmware_hardware | 23 | 3,739 | 0.62% |
| ssl_tls_crypto | 0 | 39 | 0.00% |
| other | 279 | 17,028 | 1.64% |

Sums: layer KEV totals = 887 (matches windowed total above). NVD denominators are inherited from the original analysis and are held fixed; KEV numerators update when the classifier re-runs against a new snapshot.

## Methodology

KEV is windowed to CVEs published 2021+ to align with the NVD denominator window (CVEs published 2021-2026). The numerator and denominator now agree on year scope. Per-entry layer assignment is produced by a deterministic first-match-wins classifier across fifteen layers.

- Classifier source: [data/kev-classifier.py](https://github.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/blob/main/data/kev-classifier.py)
- Per-entry classifications (JSON): [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/main/data/kev-layer-classifications.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/main/data/kev-layer-classifications.json)
- Pinned input snapshot (JSON): [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/main/data/kev-snapshot-2026-04-23.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/main/data/kev-snapshot-2026-04-23.json)
- Classifier documentation: [data/CLASSIFIER.md](https://github.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/blob/main/data/CLASSIFIER.md)
- Numeric regression test suite: [tests/](https://github.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis/blob/main/tests)

## Pages

- [Walkthrough](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/index.html): long-form analysis with sidebar navigation
- [Dashboard](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/dashboard.html): interactive Chart.js dashboard
- [Periodicity](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/periodicity.html): cross-framework backtest of the NP+DI filter
- [Build Mechanics](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/build-mechanics.html): operational layer — BAU vs floor sweeps, the cat 1/2/3 estate-maturity model (with a 1/3-1/3-1/3 worked example), WAFs as spackle, and how filter value depends on response shape
- [CVE Reference](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/cve-reference.html): per-CVE classification table
- [Glasswing](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/glasswing.html): speculative analysis of AI-discovered CVEs (intelligence assessment, not data)
- [OSV Exploitation](https://funwithscience-org.github.io/KEV-analysis/osv-exploitation.html): open-source library exploitation analysis using OSV.dev

## Caveats and limitations

- NVD denominators were derived at a prior point and may be stale. If a layer's KEV count exceeds its NVD count, that's a denominator-drift signal, not a methodology failure.
- email_collab_server at exactly 100% (44/44) is on the edge of the inherited NVD denominator. Any new email/collab KEV entry will tip the rate above 100% until the NVD denominator is refreshed.
- The classifier is rule-based and first-match-wins. Different rule orderings would produce different counts. Reproducibility is the guarantee, not objective ground truth.
- KEV is U.S.-government and enterprise-biased and undercounts open-source library exploitation. For library-specific analysis, prefer OSV.dev (which we use on the OSV Exploitation page).
- The 3-6x network-parsing lift is the conservative claim; windowed data shows 3.7-7.0x. The widened DI CWE set and the AI scan tier are documented in the periodicity page methodology.

## How to reproduce

```
git clone https://github.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis
cd KEV-analysis
python3 data/kev-classifier.py --input data/kev-snapshot-2026-04-23.json --no-snapshot
bash tests/run.sh             # full numeric regression suite
bash tests/run.sh --full      # also re-classify the pinned snapshot
```

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/funwithscience-org/KEV-analysis
- License: see repository
- Schedule: refresh agent runs nightly at 5:03 AM UTC; analyst agent at 6:10 AM UTC
